evidence.scientific_clarity: 12-study forest plot with per-study CI lines, inverse-variance-weighted square markers, dashed null line, and red diamond for pooled FE estimate (0.245 [0.196, 0.294]) fully conveys the meta-analysis claim.
evidence.typography: Script sets neither pdf.fonttype=42 nor svg.fonttype='none'; PDF embeds Type3 (bitmapped) DejaVu Sans glyphs — text is not truly editable/searchable; no font override from DejaVu Sans default.
evidence.palette: Blue (#1f77b4) for study squares, dark red (#d62728) for pooled diamond, grey (#444444) for CI lines — colorblind-safe; red reserved for pooled estimate is directionally appropriate, though red also bleeds into annotation text.
evidence.layout_density: Dynamic figure height scales with n=12; right-margin Effect [95% CI] column starts at x=484 on 569pt page leaving adequate margin; no overlapping labels detected; y-range [-0.8, n+0.6] is sensible.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure; no heterogeneity statistics (I², tau²) shown — Reviewer 2 would request these; redundant title 'Meta-analysis Forest Plot' adds no information; no legend needed.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF contains real vector path operators (m/l/h/f/B/S confirmed in decompressed stream) and BT/ET text operators, but fonts are Type3 not TrueType — fonttype=42 was not set, so glyphs are not selectable/searchable in all viewers.
evidence.file_format: All required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (24 KB), figure.png (77 KB, 1186x996 px), figure.svg (77 KB), caption.tex (5 lines), gen_figure.py (102 lines); data.json absent but not a required output.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption identifies visual encoding (square markers scaled by inverse-variance weight, horizontal CI lines), states the method (fixed-effects inverse-variance weighting), and gives a specific numerical finding (0.245, 95% CI [0.196, 0.294]).

overall_strengths: Complete, correct forest plot implementation: all required visual elements present (CI lines, weighted markers, null-effect line, pooled diamond with annotation) and the numerical claim in the caption matches the figure.
All required output files are present and non-trivial; caption is publication-quality with a specific numerical finding and clear description of visual encoding.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 not set — PDF uses Type3 (bitmapped) DejaVu Sans fonts instead of embedded TrueType, making text non-editable in PDF editors and failing the typography requirement for journal submission.
No heterogeneity statistics (I², Cochran Q, tau²) displayed, which any meta-analysis reviewer will request; the default DejaVu Sans font family is also not publication-standard.
